name: The Collapsed Passage
description: An abandoned mine tunnel, unstable and claustrophobic. The miners left in a hurry — something drove them out. Or in.

narrative.theme_description: An abandoned mine tunnel. The miners of this passage broke through into something old beneath the rock. They fled — or were taken. The corruption the town whispers about may have started here.

room_text_pool.text: Three tunnels branch from here. The left one is flooded ankle-deep. The center one has fresh claw marks on the support beams. The right one is blocked by a rusted gate.
Water seeps from cracks in the ceiling. Mining tools lie scattered as if dropped mid-swing. A corroded lockbox sits on a stone ledge above the waterline.
Picks, hammers, and coils of rope hang from hooks on the walls. A lantern still flickers on the workbench — someone was here recently.
A heavy iron gate blocks the passage. The lock is old but sturdy. Beyond it, you hear the scrape of something moving in the dark.
A cramped office carved into the rock. A desk, a chair, a journal. The air is still and heavy with dust.
The tunnel narrows. Timber supports creak overhead. Rat droppings litter the floor, growing denser ahead.
Mining carts sit on rusted tracks, overturned. Pickaxes lie scattered as if dropped mid-swing.
